evals.name: sqlalchemy_refactor_preserves_existing_api
evals.prompt: refactor the api to use sqlalchemy
evals.expected_output: The agent refactors the YOLO service database layer from raw sqlite3 calls to SQLAlchemy ORM while preserving all existing endpoint paths, status codes, and response shapes.
assertions: Creates services/yolo/models.py with PredictionSession and DetectionObject SQLAlchemy models using the existing table names prediction_sessions and detection_objects.
Creates services/yolo/db.py with create_engine, SessionLocal, get_db, and SQLite as the default database backend.
Updates services/yolo/app.py to use FastAPI Depends(get_db) and SQLAlchemy Session for database access.
Removes raw application database usage from services/yolo/app.py, including import sqlite3, DB_PATH, init_db(), CREATE TABLE, INSERT INTO, and SELECT SQL strings.
Preserves /predict response keys exactly: prediction_uid, detection_count, labels, and time_took.
Preserves existing 400 and 404 error details for invalid files, missing predictions, missing images, empty labels, and invalid score ranges.
Updates services/yolo/tests to use SQLAlchemy models or sessions instead of sqlite3, DB_PATH monkeypatching, or init_db().
Adds sqlalchemy to services/yolo/requirements.txt.
Runs pytest tests/ from services/yolo and reports the result.

evals.name: recent_predictions_endpoint
evals.prompt: add an endpoint GET /predictions/recent that returns the 10 most recent sessions
evals.expected_output: The agent adds a recent-predictions endpoint backed by the SQLAlchemy data layer and tests that it returns the newest prediction sessions first with a maximum of 10 results.
assertions: Ensures services/yolo/models.py and services/yolo/db.py exist and are used for persistence.
Adds GET /predictions/recent in services/yolo/app.py without breaking existing /prediction/{uid}, /predictions/label/{label}, or /predictions/score/{min_score} routes.
Queries PredictionSession with SQLAlchemy, orders by PredictionSession.timestamp descending, and limits results to 10.
Formats timestamp values as YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS to avoid changing the API timestamp shape.
Adds tests that insert more than 10 PredictionSession rows through SQLAlchemy and assert only the 10 newest are returned.
Does not use raw sqlite3, raw SELECT strings, or DB_PATH for the new endpoint.
Runs pytest tests/ from services/yolo and reports the result.

evals.name: user_feedback_table
evals.prompt: add a UserFeedback table to track user ratings per prediction
evals.expected_output: The agent extends the SQLAlchemy model layer with a UserFeedback table related to prediction sessions, plus API and tests if endpoints are needed by the requested behavior.
assertions: Adds a UserFeedback SQLAlchemy model in services/yolo/models.py with a table name such as user_feedback.
Defines a foreign key from UserFeedback to prediction_sessions.uid.
Uses explicit columns for at least id, prediction_uid, rating, and a timestamp or created_at field; includes comment text only if useful for the feature.
Uses SQLAlchemy relationships only where they keep the code readable for students.
Creates tables through Base.metadata.create_all rather than manual CREATE TABLE SQL.
Adds or updates tests that create feedback for an existing prediction through SQLAlchemy-backed app behavior.
Does not reintroduce sqlite3, DB_PATH, init_db(), or raw INSERT/SELECT SQL.
Runs pytest tests/ from services/yolo and reports the result.

evals.name: predict_endpoint_tests
evals.expected_output: The agent writes or updates /predict tests that work with the SQLAlchemy data layer and verify both the HTTP response and persisted database rows.
assertions: Uses FastAPI TestClient to call POST /predict with a real test image from services/yolo/tests/data.
Asserts the response includes prediction_uid, detection_count, labels, and time_took with compatible value types.
Verifies a PredictionSession row is persisted using a SQLAlchemy Session, not sqlite3.
Verifies DetectionObject rows are persisted for the prediction uid using SQLAlchemy model queries.
Keeps the existing non-image upload test and its exact 400 detail: Only image files are supported.
Uses dependency_overrides for get_db or an equivalent explicit SQLAlchemy test database setup.
Does not call init_db() or monkeypatch app.DB_PATH.
Runs pytest tests/ from services/yolo and reports the result.

evals.name: architectural_database_fix
evals.prompt: the database layer doesn't follow our architectural design, fix it
evals.expected_output: The agent identifies the raw SQLite data-access code as the architecture issue and refactors it into explicit SQLAlchemy model and database modules while keeping the service behavior unchanged.
assertions: Creates or uses services/yolo/models.py for declarative SQLAlchemy models.
Creates or uses services/yolo/db.py for database URL configuration, engine, SessionLocal, and get_db.
Keeps route handlers explicit and readable, with database sessions passed through Depends(get_db).
Removes raw sqlite3 from application code and avoids hiding behavior behind a high-level framework or opaque abstraction.
Keeps the image-processing flow in /predict intact: validate extension, save original image, run YOLO, save annotated image, persist rows, return the same response shape.
Updates tests to follow the new architecture instead of reaching into raw SQLite internals.
Runs pytest tests/ from services/yolo and reports the result.

evals.name: delete_prediction_cascade
evals.prompt: delete a prediction session and all its detection objects by uid
evals.expected_output: The agent adds delete-by-uid behavior using SQLAlchemy ORM deletion and cascade behavior so both the prediction session and its detection objects are removed.
assertions: Defines a relationship between PredictionSession and DetectionObject with cascade behavior such as cascade="all, delete-orphan".
Adds a DELETE endpoint for a prediction uid only if one does not already exist and chooses a clear path consistent with existing prediction routes.
Returns 404 with a clear detail when the prediction uid does not exist.
Deletes the PredictionSession through the SQLAlchemy Session and commits the transaction.
Tests that deleting an existing prediction removes both the PredictionSession row and all related DetectionObject rows.
Tests the missing-uid 404 path.
Does not use raw DELETE SQL, sqlite3, DB_PATH, or manual cleanup queries.
Runs pytest tests/ from services/yolo and reports the result.

evals.name: processing_time_column
evals.prompt: add a column processing_time_ms to the prediction_sessions table
evals.expected_output: The agent adds a processing_time_ms SQLAlchemy model column, persists it during /predict, and keeps the existing /predict response shape unless explicitly asked to change it.
assertions: Adds processing_time_ms to PredictionSession in services/yolo/models.py using an appropriate numeric column type such as Integer or Float.
Computes processing time in /predict and persists processing_time_ms on the PredictionSession row.
Keeps the existing /predict response key time_took unchanged.
Does not remove or rename existing PredictionSession columns uid, timestamp, original_image, or predicted_image.
Adds tests that assert processing_time_ms is saved in the database after POST /predict.
Updates any response tests only if the API already returns session details that should now include the new column.
Does not use ALTER TABLE SQL, sqlite3, DB_PATH, or init_db().
Runs pytest tests/ from services/yolo and reports the result.

evals.name: postgres_configurable_backend
evals.prompt: make the database backend configurable so we can use postgres in production
evals.expected_output: The agent adds environment-driven SQLAlchemy database configuration that defaults to SQLite and switches to Postgres when DB_BACKEND=postgres.
assertions: Creates or updates services/yolo/db.py to read DB_BACKEND with a default value of sqlite.
Uses sqlite:///./predictions.db or DATABASE_URL for local SQLite by default.
Builds a Postgres SQLAlchemy URL from environment variables such as DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_HOST, DB_PORT, and DB_NAME when DB_BACKEND=postgres.
Uses connect_args={"check_same_thread": False} only for SQLite.
Adds a Postgres driver such as psycopg2-binary to services/yolo/requirements.txt.
Documents the new database environment variables in services/yolo/README.md.
Keeps tests running against temporary SQLite with SQLAlchemy dependency overrides.
Does not hardcode production credentials.
Runs pytest tests/ from services/yolo and reports the result.
